Implementation of options builder API.

void ensure_dependency_capacity (options_builder_t * builder)

Grow dependency array if needed.

Definition at line 224 of file builder.c.

224                                                             {
225   if (builder->num_dependencies >= builder->dependency_capacity) {
226     size_t new_capacity = builder->dependency_capacity * 2;
227     option_dependency_t *new_dependencies =
228         SAFE_REALLOC(builder->dependencies, new_capacity * sizeof(option_dependency_t), option_dependency_t *);
229     if (!new_dependencies) {
230       log_fatal("Failed to reallocate dependencies array");
231       return;
232     }
233     builder->dependencies = new_dependencies;
234     builder->dependency_capacity = new_capacity;
235   }
236 }

const option_descriptor_t * find_option_descriptor (const
  options_config_t * config, const char * opt_name)


Find option descriptor by short or long name.


Definition at line 1487 of file builder.c.


1487                                                                                                         {
1488   if (!opt_name || opt_name[0] == '\0')
1489     return NULL;
1490 
1491   // Handle short options: -x (single dash, single char)
1492   if (opt_name[0] == '-' && opt_name[1] != '-' && opt_name[1] != '\0') {
1493     // This is a short option like -x
1494     char short_char = opt_name[1];
1495     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1496       if (config->descriptors[i].short_name == short_char) {
1497         return &config->descriptors[i];
1498       }
1499     }
1500     // Short option not found
1501     return NULL;
1502   }
1503 
1504   // Handle long options: --name (double dash)
1505   if (opt_name[0] == '-' && opt_name[1] == '-' && opt_name[2] != '\0') {
1506     const char *long_name = opt_name + 2; // Skip the '--'
1507     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1508       if (strcmp(config->descriptors[i].long_name, long_name) == 0) {
1509         return &config->descriptors[i];
1510       }
1511     }
1512     // Long option not found
1513     return NULL;
1514   }
1515 
1516   // Not a recognized option format
1517   return NULL;
1518 }

int
  format_option_default_value_str (const option_descriptor_t * desc, char
  * buf, size_t bufsize)


Format option default value as a string.


Parameters


desc Option descriptor


buf Output buffer


bufsize Size of buffer


Returns


Number of characters written to buf


Definition at line 77 of file builder.c.


77                                                                                                 {
78   if (!desc || !buf || bufsize == 0) {
79     return 0;
80   }
81 
82   // For callback options with enums, look up the enum string by matching the default value
83   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->metadata.enum_values && desc->default_value) {
84     int default_int_val = 0;
85     memcpy(&default_int_val, desc->default_value, sizeof(int));
86 
87     // Try to find matching enum value
88     if (desc->metadata.enum_integer_values) {
89       for (size_t i = 0; desc->metadata.enum_values[i] != NULL; i++) {
90         if (desc->metadata.enum_integer_values[i] == default_int_val) {
91           return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", desc->metadata.enum_values[i]);
92         }
93       }
94     } else {
95       // Fallback: assume sequential 0-based indices if integer values not provided
96       // Count enum values to check bounds
97       size_t enum_count = 0;
98       while (desc->metadata.enum_values[enum_count] != NULL) {
99         enum_count++;
100       }
101       if (default_int_val >= 0 && (size_t)default_int_val < enum_count) {
102         return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", desc->metadata.enum_values[default_int_val]);
103       }
104     }
105   }
106 
107   // For callback options storing numeric types (int/double/float), format them as numbers
108   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->default_value && desc->metadata.enum_values == NULL) {
109     // Check if this is a numeric callback by looking for numeric_range metadata
110     // Numeric callbacks have min/max range constraints set (max != 0 indicates a range was set)
111     if (desc->metadata.numeric_range.max != 0 || desc->metadata.numeric_range.min != 0) {
112       // This is a numeric callback option - extract and format the value
113       // Determine if it's int or double based on range (int max is 2147483647)
114       double default_double = 0.0;
115       if (desc->metadata.numeric_range.max <= 2147483647.0 && desc->metadata.numeric_range.min >= -2147483648.0) {
116         // Integer-ranged value (like port 1-65535) - stored as int
117         int default_int = 0;
118         memcpy(&default_int, desc->default_value, sizeof(int));
119         default_double = (double)default_int;
120       } else {
121         // Double-ranged value - stored as double
122         memcpy(&default_double, desc->default_value, sizeof(double));
123       }
124 
125       // Format with appropriate precision (remove trailing zeros for integers)
126       char formatted[32];
127       safe_snprintf(formatted, sizeof(formatted), "%.1f", default_double);
128 
129       // Remove trailing zeros after decimal point
130       char *dot = strchr(formatted, '.');
131       if (dot) {
132         char *end = formatted + strlen(formatted) - 1;
133         while (end > dot && *end == '0') {
134           end--;
135         }
136         if (*end == '.') {
137           *end = '\0';
138         } else {
139           *(end + 1) = '\0';
140         }
141       }
142 
143       return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", formatted);
144     }
145   }
146 
147   // For callback options with string defaults (no enum, no numeric range)
148   // Treat default_value as a const char* string pointer
149   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->default_value && desc->metadata.enum_values == NULL &&
150       desc->metadata.numeric_range.max == 0 && desc->metadata.numeric_range.min == 0) {
151     const char *str_default = (const char *)desc->default_value;
152     if (str_default && str_default[0] != '\0') {
153       return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", str_default);
154     }
155   }
156 
157   return options_format_default_value(desc->type, desc->default_value, buf, bufsize);
158 }

const
  char * get_option_help_placeholder_str (const option_descriptor_t *
  desc)


Get help placeholder string for an option type.


Parameters


desc Option descriptor


Returns


Pointer to string literal ("INTEGER",
  "NUMBER", "STRING", "VALUE",
  "[BOOLEAN]"), custom placeholder, or empty string


Definition at line 37 of file builder.c.


37                                                                              {
38   if (!desc) {
39     return "";
40   }
41   // Check for custom placeholder first
42   if (desc->arg_placeholder != NULL) {
43     return desc->arg_placeholder;
44   }
45 
46   // For callback options, check if it's a boolean based on enum values
47   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->metadata.enum_values) {
48     // Check if enum values contain "true" and "false" (boolean-like)
49     const char *const *values = desc->metadata.enum_values;
50     bool has_true = false, has_false = false;
51 
52     for (int i = 0; values[i] != NULL; i++) {
53       if (strcmp(values[i], "true") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "yes") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "on") == 0) {
54         has_true = true;
55       }
56       if (strcmp(values[i], "false") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "no") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "off") == 0) {
57         has_false = true;
58       }
59     }
60 
61     // If it has both true-like and false-like values, it's boolean
62     if (has_true && has_false) {
63       return "BOOLEAN";
64     }
65   }
66 
67   return options_get_type_placeholder(desc->type);
68 }

bool option_applies_to_mode (const
  option_descriptor_t * desc, asciichat_mode_t mode, bool
  for_binary_help)


Check if an option applies to a specific mode using bitmask.


Parameters


desc Option descriptor


mode Mode to check (use invalid value like -1 for binary help)


for_binary_help If true, show binary options; if false, hide them


Returns


true if option should be shown for this mode


Definition at line 171 of file builder.c.


171                                                                                                           {
172   if (!desc) {
173     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Descriptor is NULL");
174     return false;
175   }
176 
177   // When for_binary_help is true (i.e., for 'ascii-chat --help'),
178   // show options that apply to the default mode (DISCOVERY) or binary-level options only.
179   // Don't show mode-specific options for other modes (server, client, mirror, discovery-service).
180   if (for_binary_help) {
181     // Binary-level options or options that apply to discovery mode (the default)
182     option_mode_bitmask_t default_modes = OPTION_MODE_BINARY | OPTION_MODE_DISCOVERY;
183     return (desc->mode_bitmask & default_modes) != 0 && !desc->hide_from_binary_help;
184   }
185 
186   // For mode-specific help (non-discovery modes), show only options for that mode.
187   // Do not show binary options here unless it also specifically applies to the mode.
188   // The 'mode' parameter is the actual mode (e.g., MODE_CLIENT, MODE_SERVER).
189   if (mode < 0 || mode > MODE_DISCOVERY) { // Use MODE_INVALID as upper bound for valid modes
190     return false;
191   }
192   option_mode_bitmask_t mode_bit = (1 << mode);
193 
194   // Check if it's a binary option. If so, only show if it also explicitly applies to this mode.
195   if ((desc->mode_bitmask & OPTION_MODE_BINARY) && !(desc->mode_bitmask & mode_bit)) {
196     return false; // Binary options not shown in mode-specific help unless also mode-specific
197   }
198 
199   bool applies = (desc->mode_bitmask & mode_bit) != 0;
200 
201   return applies && !desc->hide_from_mode_help;
202 }

    option_descriptor_t::hide_from_binary_help,

    option_descriptor_t::mode_bitmask, MODE_DISCOVERY,
    OPTION_MODE_BINARY, OPTION_MODE_DISCOVERY, and

void
  options_builder_add_action (options_builder_t * builder, const char *
  long_name, char short_name, void(* action_fn )(void), const char * help_text,
  const char * group)


Add action option (executes action and may exit). Action options
    execute a callback function when encountered during parsing. The callback
    may exit the program (e.g., --list-webcams, --version).




Parameters


builder Builder to add to


long_name Long option name


short_name Short option char (or '\0')


action_fn Action callback to execute


help_text Help description


group Group name for help


Definition at line 942 of file builder.c.


943                                                                                                    {
944   ensure_descriptor_capacity(builder);
945 
946   option_descriptor_t desc = {.long_name = long_name,
947                               .short_name = short_name,
948                               .type = OPTION_TYPE_ACTION,
949                               .offset = 0, // Actions don't store values
950                               .help_text = help_text,
951                               .group = group,
952                               .default_value = NULL,
953                               .required = false, // Actions are never required
954                               .env_var_name = NULL,
955                               .validate = NULL,
956                               .parse_fn = NULL,
957                               .action_fn = action_fn,
958                               .owns_memory = false,
959                               .hide_from_mode_help = false,
960                               .hide_from_binary_help = false,
961                               .mode_bitmask = OPTION_MODE_NONE};
962 
963   builder->descriptors[builder->num_descriptors++] = desc;
964 
965   // Set hide_from_binary_help after adding (so we can check the option name)

  options_builder_add_example (options_builder_t * builder,

Add example descriptor. Adds an example command to be printed in
    the EXAMPLES section. Components are colored separately during printing:


    
  
  • mode: magenta
    • 
  
  • args: green
    • 



Parameters


builder Builder instance


mode Mode name (NULL or "server", "client",
  "mirror", etc.)


args Arguments (NULL or "example.com",
  "swift-river-mountain", etc.)


description Help text for this example


Example:




options_builder_add_example(b, NULL, NULL,


                            "Start new session");
options_builder_add_example(b, "server", NULL,


                            "Run as dedicated server");
options_builder_add_example(b, "client", "example.com",


                            "Connect to specific server");



Add an example to the help output




Parameters


builder Builder instance


mode_bitmask Bitmask of modes (OPTION_MODE_SERVER | OPTION_MODE_CLIENT,
  etc.) Use OPTION_MODE_BINARY for binary-level examples


args Arguments (NULL or "example.com",
  etc.)


description Help text for this example


owns_args If true, duplicate and track the args string

1262                                   .description = description,
1263                                   .owns_args_memory = owns_args,
1264                                   .is_utility_command = false};
1265 
1266   if (owns_args) {
1267     // If owning, duplicate the string and track it

1270       log_fatal("Failed to duplicate example args string");
1271       return;
1272     }
1273     example.args = owned_args;
1274     track_owned_string_builder(builder, owned_args);
1275   } else {
1276     example.args = args;
1277   }
1278 
1279   builder->examples[builder->num_examples++] = example;
1280 }

void
  options_builder_add_positional (options_builder_t * builder, const char
  * name, const char * help_text, bool required, const char *
  section_heading, const char ** examples, size_t num_examples,
  option_mode_bitmask_t mode_bitmask, int(* parse_fn )(const char *arg,
  void *config, char **remaining, int num_remaining, char **error_msg))


Add positional argument descriptor. Positional arguments are
    parsed after getopt finishes, from the remaining argv elements that don't
    start with '-'. They are parsed in the order they are added to the
  builder.




Example (client mode):




options_builder_add_positional(


    builder,


    "address",


    "[address][:port] - Server address (IPv4, IPv6, or hostname) with optional port",


    false,  // Not required (defaults to localhost)


    parse_client_address_arg
);



Example (server mode):




options_builder_add_positional(


    builder,


    "bind-addr",


    "Bind address (IPv4 or IPv6) - can specify 0-2 addresses",


    false,  // Not required (defaults to localhost dual-stack)


    parse_server_bind_addr
);



Parameters


builder Builder to add to


name Name for help text (e.g., "address")


help_text Description for usage message


required If true, this positional arg must be provided


section_heading Optional section heading for examples (e.g.,
  "ADDRESS FORMATS")


examples Optional array of example strings with descriptions


num_examples Number of examples in array


mode_bitmask Which modes this positional arg applies to


parse_fn Custom parser (receives arg, config, remaining args, error_msg)
  Returns number of args consumed (usually 1), or -1 on error


Definition at line 1191 of file builder.c.


1195                                                                        {
1196   if (!builder || !name || !parse_fn)
1197     return;
1198 
1199   ensure_positional_arg_capacity(builder);
1200 
1201   positional_arg_descriptor_t pos_arg = {.name = name,
1202                                          .help_text = help_text,
1203                                          .required = required,
1204                                          .section_heading = section_heading,
1205                                          .examples = examples,
1206                                          .num_examples = num_examples,
1207                                          .mode_bitmask = mode_bitmask,
1208                                          .parse_fn = parse_fn};
1209 
1210   builder->positional_args[builder->num_positional_args++] = pos_arg;
1211 }

Add string option. Strings are automatically strdup'd during

builder Builder to add to


long_name Long option name


short_name Short option char (or '\0')


offset offsetof(struct, field)


default_value Default value (will be strdup'd, or NULL)


help_text Help description


group Group name for help


required If true, option must be provided


env_var_name Environment variable fallback (or NULL)


validate Validator function receiving full struct (or NULL)

749   static size_t num_defaults = 0;
750 
751   const void *default_ptr = NULL;
752   if (default_value) {
753     if (num_defaults >= 1024) {
754       log_error("Too many string options (max 1024)");
755       return;
756     }
757     defaults[num_defaults] = default_value;
758     default_ptr = &defaults[num_defaults++];
759   }
760

762                               .short_name = short_name,

771                               .parse_fn = NULL,
772                               .owns_memory = true, // Strings are always owned

void
  options_builder_add_usage (options_builder_t * builder, const char *
  mode, const char * positional, bool show_options, const char *
  description)


Add usage line descriptor. Adds a usage line to be printed in the
    USAGE section. Components are colored separately during printing:


    
  
  • mode: magenta
    • 
  
  • positional: green
    • 
  
  • "[options...]": yellow
    • 



Parameters


builder Builder instance


mode Mode name (NULL for binary-level, or "server",
  "<mode>", etc.)


positional Positional args (NULL or "[bind-addr]",
  "<session-string>", etc.)


show_options True to append "[options...]" or
  "[mode-options...]"


description Help text for this usage pattern


Example:




options_builder_add_usage(b, NULL, NULL, true,


                          "Start a new session");
options_builder_add_usage(b, NULL, "<session-string>", true,


                          "Join an existing session");
options_builder_add_usage(b, "<mode>", NULL, true,


                          "Run in a specific mode");

1249       .mode = mode, .positional = positional, .show_options = show_options, .description = description};
1250 
1251   builder->usage_lines[builder->num_usage_lines++] = usage;
1252 }

New builder (must be freed with

425     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Preset config is NULL");

427   }
428 
429   options_builder_t *builder = options_builder_create(preset->struct_size);
430   if (!builder) {
431     return NULL;

433 
434   builder->program_name = preset->program_name;
435   builder->description = preset->description;
436 
437   // Copy all descriptors
438   for (size_t i = 0; i < preset->num_descriptors; i++) {
439     options_builder_add_descriptor(builder, &preset->descriptors[i]);
440   }
441 
442   // Copy all dependencies
443   for (size_t i = 0; i < preset->num_dependencies; i++) {
444     options_builder_add_dependency(builder, &preset->dependencies[i]);
445   }
446 
447   // Copy all positional arguments
448   for (size_t i = 0; i < preset->num_positional_args; i++) {
449     const positional_arg_descriptor_t *pos_arg = &preset->positional_args[i];

void
  options_builder_mark_binary_only (options_builder_t * builder, const
  char * option_name)


Mark an option as binary-level only (hide from mode-specific
    help). Binary-level options are still parsed by mode-specific parsers (so
    they work anywhere in the command line), but they don't appear in
    mode-specific --help. They should only be documented in the top-level binary
    help.




Parameters


builder Builder containing the option


option_name Long name of the option to mark


Example:




options_builder_add_string(b, "log-file", 'L', ...);
options_builder_mark_binary_only(b, "log-file");



Definition at line 1171 of file builder.c.


1171                                                                                            {
1172   if (!builder || !option_name)
1173     return;
1174 
1175   // Find the option by name and mark it
1176   for (size_t i = 0; i < builder->num_descriptors; i++) {
1177     if (strcmp(builder->descriptors[i].long_name, option_name) == 0) {
1178       builder->descriptors[i].hide_from_mode_help = true;
1179       return;
1180     }
1181   }
1182 
1183   // Option not found - this is a programming error
1184   log_warn("Attempted to mark non-existent option '%s' as binary-only", option_name);
1185 }

void
  options_builder_set_arg_placeholder (options_builder_t * builder, const
  char * arg_placeholder)


Set custom argument placeholder on the last added option
    descriptor. Sets the arg_placeholder field on the most recently added option
    descriptor. This allows customizing the placeholder text shown in help
    (e.g., "SHELL [FILE]" instead of "STR").




Parameters


builder Options builder


arg_placeholder Custom placeholder text (e.g., "SHELL [FILE]"),

    "16 colors (ANSI)",


    "256 colors (xterm)",


    "24-bit truecolor (modern terminals)"

asciichat_error_t options_config_parse_positional (const
  options_config_t * config, int remaining_argc, char ** remaining_argv,
  void * options_struct)


Parse positional arguments. Parses positional arguments from
    remaining_argv after getopt processing. Calls each positional arg
    descriptor's parse_fn in order.




This is called automatically by options_config_parse(), but
    can also be called separately if you want custom control over the parsing
    flow.




Parameters


Config Module Options
  configuration


remaining_argc Count of remaining args


remaining_argv Array of remaining args


options_struct Options struct to fill


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_USAGE on parse
  errors


Definition at line 1330 of file builder.c.


1331                                                                                                {
1332   if (!config || !options_struct) {
1333     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Config or options struct is NULL");
1334   }
1335 
1336   if (remaining_argc < 0 || (remaining_argc > 0 && !remaining_argv)) {
1337     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid remaining args");
1338   }
1339 
1340   // No positional args defined - but we got some positional arguments
1341   if (config->num_positional_args == 0 && remaining_argc > 0) {
1342     log_error("Error: Unexpected positional argument '%s'", remaining_argv[0]);
1343     return ERROR_USAGE;
1344   }
1345 
1346   // Check if required positional args are missing
1347   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_positional_args; i++) {
1348     const positional_arg_descriptor_t *pos_arg = &config->positional_args[i];
1349     if (pos_arg->required && remaining_argc == 0) {
1350       log_error("Error: Missing required positional argument '%s'", pos_arg->name);
1351       if (pos_arg->help_text) {
1352         log_error("  %s", pos_arg->help_text);
1353       }
1354       return ERROR_USAGE;
1355     }
1356   }
1357 
1358   // Parse positional arguments
1359   int arg_index = 0;
1360   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_positional_args && arg_index < remaining_argc; i++) {
1361     const positional_arg_descriptor_t *pos_arg = &config->positional_args[i];
1362 
1363     const char *arg = remaining_argv[arg_index];
1364     char **remaining = (arg_index + 1 < remaining_argc) ? &remaining_argv[arg_index + 1] : NULL;
1365     int num_remaining = remaining_argc - arg_index - 1;
1366     char *error_msg = NULL;
1367 
1368     // Call custom parser
1369     int consumed = pos_arg->parse_fn(arg, options_struct, remaining, num_remaining, &error_msg);
1370 
1371     if (consumed < 0) {
1372       // Parse error
1373       if (error_msg) {
1374         log_error("Error parsing positional argument '%s': %s", pos_arg->name, error_msg);
1375         free(error_msg);
1376       } else {
1377         log_error("Error parsing positional argument '%s': %s", pos_arg->name, arg);
1378       }
1379       return ERROR_USAGE;
1380     }
1381 
1382     // Advance by consumed args (usually 1, but can be more for multi-arg parsers)
1383     arg_index += consumed;
1384   }
1385 
1386   // Check for extra unconsumed positional arguments
1387   if (arg_index < remaining_argc) {
1388     log_error("Error: Unexpected positional argument '%s'", remaining_argv[arg_index]);
1389     return ERROR_USAGE;
1390   }
1391 
1392   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1393 }

Parameters


Config Module Options

options_struct Options struct to initialize


Returns

1399                                                                                                     {
1400   if (!config || !options_struct) {
1401     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Config or options struct is NULL");
1402   }
1403 
1404   char *base = (char *)options_struct;
1405 
1406   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1407     const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
1408     void *field = base + desc->offset;
1409 
1410     // Check environment variable first (primary)
1411     const char *env_value = NULL;
1412     if (desc->env_var_name) {
1413       env_value = SAFE_GETENV(desc->env_var_name);
1414     }
1415     // Check shorter alias names as fallback if primary not set
1416     if (!env_value) {
1417       if (desc->env_var_name && strcmp(desc->env_var_name, "ASCII_CHAT_PORT") == 0) {
1418         env_value = SAFE_GETENV("PORT");
1419       } else if (desc->env_var_name && strcmp(desc->env_var_name, "ASCII_CHAT_WEBSOCKET_PORT") == 0) {
1420         env_value = SAFE_GETENV("WS_PORT");
1421       }
1422     }
1423 
1424     // Use handler registry to apply environment variables and defaults
1425     if (desc->type >= 0 && desc->type < (int)(NUM_OPTION_TYPES)) {
1426       if (g_builder_handlers[desc->type].apply_env) {
1427         g_builder_handlers[desc->type].apply_env(field, env_value, desc);
1428       }
1429     } else if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->parse_fn && desc->default_value) {
1430       // Special handling for callbacks with parse_fn
1431       char *error_msg = NULL;
1432       desc->parse_fn(NULL, field, &error_msg);
1433       if (error_msg) {
1434         free(error_msg);
1435       }
1436     }
1437   }
1438 
1439   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1440 }

asciichat_error_t options_config_validate (const
  options_config_t * config, const void * options_struct, char **
  error_message)


Validate options struct. Checks:


    
  
  • Required fields are set
    • 
  
  • Dependencies are satisfied
    • 
  
  • Custom validators pass
    • 



Parameters


Config Module Options
  configuration


options_struct Options struct to validate


error_message Optional: receives detailed error message (must free)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if valid, error code otherwise


Definition at line 1837 of file builder.c.


1838                                                                 {
1839   if (!config || !options_struct) {
1840     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Config or options struct is NULL");
1841   }
1842 
1843   const char *base = (const char *)options_struct;
1844 
1845   // Check required fields
1846   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1847     const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
1848     if (!desc->required)
1849       continue;
1850 
1851     const void *field = base + desc->offset;
1852     // Use handler registry to check if option is set (for required field validation)
1853     bool is_set = true;
1854     if (desc->type >= 0 && desc->type < (int)(NUM_OPTION_TYPES)) {
1855       if (g_builder_handlers[desc->type].is_set) {
1856         is_set = g_builder_handlers[desc->type].is_set(field, desc);
1857       }
1858     }
1859 
1860     if (!is_set) {
1861       if (error_message) {
1862         int asprintf_result;
1863         if (desc->env_var_name) {
1864           asprintf_result = asprintf(error_message, "Required option --%s is not set (set %s env var or use --%s)", desc->long_name,
1865                                      desc->env_var_name, desc->long_name);
1866         } else {
1867           asprintf_result = asprintf(error_message, "Required option --%s is not set", desc->long_name);
1868         }
1869         if (asprintf_result < 0) {
1870           log_error("Failed to format error message for missing required option --%s", desc->long_name);
1871           *error_message = NULL;
1872         }
1873       }
1874       return ERROR_USAGE;
1875     }
1876   }
1877 
1878   // Check dependencies
1879   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_dependencies; i++) {
1880     const option_dependency_t *dep = &config->dependencies[i];
1881 
1882     bool option_is_set = is_option_set(config, options_struct, dep->option_name);
1883     bool depends_is_set = is_option_set(config, options_struct, dep->depends_on);
1884 
1885     switch (dep->type) {
1886     case DEPENDENCY_REQUIRES:
1887       if (option_is_set && !depends_is_set) {
1888         if (error_message) {
1889           if (dep->error_message) {
1890             *error_message = platform_strdup(dep->error_message);
1891           } else {
1892             int asprintf_result = asprintf(error_message, "Option --%s requires --%s to be set", dep->option_name, dep->depends_on);
1893             if (asprintf_result < 0) {
1894               log_error("Failed to format error message for option dependency: --%s requires --%s", dep->option_name, dep->depends_on);
1895               *error_message = NULL;
1896             }
1897           }
1898         }
1899         return ERROR_USAGE;
1900       }
1901       break;
1902 
1903     case DEPENDENCY_CONFLICTS:
1904       if (option_is_set && depends_is_set) {
1905         if (error_message) {
1906           if (dep->error_message) {
1907             *error_message = platform_strdup(dep->error_message);
1908           } else {
1909             int asprintf_result = asprintf(error_message, "Option --%s conflicts with --%s", dep->option_name, dep->depends_on);
1910             if (asprintf_result < 0) {
1911               log_error("Failed to format error message for option conflict: --%s conflicts with --%s", dep->option_name, dep->depends_on);
1912               *error_message = NULL;
1913             }
1914           }
1915         }
1916         return ERROR_USAGE;
1917       }
1918       break;
1919 
1920     case DEPENDENCY_IMPLIES:
1921       // Implies is handled during parsing, not validation
1922       break;
1923     }
1924   }
1925 
1926   // Run custom validators
1927   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1928     const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
1929     if (!desc->validate)
1930       continue;
1931 
1932     char *custom_error = NULL;
1933     if (!desc->validate(options_struct, &custom_error)) {
1934       if (error_message) {
1935         *error_message = custom_error;
1936       } else {
1937         free(custom_error);
1938       }
1939       return ERROR_USAGE;
1940     }
1941   }
1942 
1943   // Cross-field validation: Check for conflicting color options
1944   // Cannot use --color with --color-mode none
1945   const options_t *opts = (const options_t *)options_struct;
1946   if (opts->color && opts->color_mode == TERM_COLOR_NONE) {
1947     if (error_message) {
1948       int asprintf_result = asprintf(error_message, "Option --color cannot be used with --color-mode=none (conflicting color settings)");
1949       if (asprintf_result < 0) {
1950         log_error("Failed to format error message for color option conflict");
1951         *error_message = NULL;
1952       }
1953     }
1954     return ERROR_USAGE;
1955   }
1956 
1957   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1958 }
